title: Phyllorkis caespitosa
taxonomy: Phyllorkis caespitosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. This species is part of the genus Phyllorkis, which is closely related to the genus Pseudobulbophyllum. Within the Orchidaceae family, Phyllorkis caespitosa is placed in the subfamily Epidendroideae. The classification of Phyllorkis caespitosa is further divided into various ranks, including kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species.

intro: Phyllorkis caespitosa, also known as the 'orchid tree', is a tropical plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, woody habit and orchid-like flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
